✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-31 17:26:31
Hudson Williams via instagram story. The painting is “La Blanchisseuse” (The Laundress) by Henri de Toulouse-Lautrec. This painting showcases the gritty life and working conditions endured by the working classes throughout the 19th century. The subject of laundresses, also known as washerwomen, was a popular one in art, especially in France.

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-31 16:59:38
Nothing better than a Hudson Williams film festival to celebrate our favorite cinephile's birthday! Throughout the month of February, we'll be watching his audiovisual projects each day and discussing them! Every day, we will post a tweet asking about your favorite parts and you can share insights on his work so we can discuss, engage with his projects, and have fun at the same time! The image below shows which short film we'll watch on each day, and here's the playlist to watch on YouTube: 🔗 https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLdQDCenFFf1qeBAVeitI1FKZCXN9JFZC_&si=-T8PBpRbu8E9TsUv

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 16:27:52
Connor Storrie on his matching tattoo with Hudson Williams (via @TeenVogue) “When I went to Vancouver, I went to visit Hudson for a week. I didn’t realize it, but from L.A. to Vancouver, it’s like two hours and 300 bucks, so I’m going out there all the time now. We knew we wanted to get a little tattoo together, just to commemorate our connection and being on the show because it’s pretty momentous for both of us. We really are just the best of friends, so we were just like, “Let’s do it.” We had so many ideas before “SEX SELLS.” […] We didn’t want to do anything that was too Heated Rivalry on the nose, and I think I came up with “SEX SELLS.” […] I like it because it’s on the surface provocative. It’s kind of like, “Oh, what is that?” But at the end of the day, we got to meet each other and got to have, for me, one of the coolest experiences of my life doing something on the surface that is based in sex, and what we get out of that, and what people find attractive about that. It’s meta for me in that sense where it’s, on the surface, provocative. But underneath it, it’s about connection and experience. For me, when I make decisions like that, I just go with the impulse and I’m like, “This is what we have to do.” And then retrospectively, I can be like, “Oh yeah, that is why that felt correct.” But in the moment, I think we were both just like, “SEX SELLS,” and we were like, “Yep, yep. That’s it.” 🔗 https://www.teenvogue.com/story/pre-fame-hudson-williams-connor-storrie-interview-heated-rivalry

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 13:43:08
Hudson Williams on why he thinks Shane and Ilya were so drawn to each other (via @TeenVogue) "Ilya is his counterpart and is sort of like his inverse. He’s just as good as Shane [at hockey] and sometimes threatens him — maybe he’s better than him, but he goes about it in a completely different way. I think some of it helps Shane see hockey and life in just a different way, that there’s another way to go about it that’s so different. They’ve arrived at the same spot [professionally], but Ilya is the party animal; he’s the playboy. Shane is just pure discipline and focus. I think someone else proving that you can go about it a different way is just so alluring to Shane, both as a person and a competitor."

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 13:38:49
Hudson Williams on what he's most excited to act from "The Long Game" in "Heated Rivalry" season 2 (via @TeenVogue) "I would say a particular scene with Hayden’s kids would be really rewarding. [Storrie nods.] And one scene I just want to get knee deep in and pull up the gloves on is the commissioner scenes. I want to get in that room. I want to be in front of whoever’s cast as that commissioner. I want to seethe. I want to see red. I’m going to punch him in the mouth. It’s going to be unscripted. The actor won’t see it coming. I feel bad for whoever it is. Jacob will disapprove." 🔗 https://www.teenvogue.com/story/pre-fame-hudson-williams-connor-storrie-interview-heated-rivalry

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 13:36:03
Hudson Williams on people dismissing "Heated Rivalry" because of the sex scenes (via @TeenVogue) "I kind of take issue with people writing off sex on TV. I’ll see those comments. I’ll see the people kind of going, 'Oh, is there really a need to show this?' And I’ve always had a problem with that, because, of course, there are the times when it’s low-hanging fruit, and people are just using it to sell something and just smack it on there. But since cinema was created, there have been beautiful, usually European filmmakers, a lot of Asian filmmakers, that have a very sex-forward view. Like this show, I also think of Normal People. The sex was a part of driving the story forward. It wasn’t gratuitous. It was real, it was beautiful, sometimes ugly, but always really story-focused. I feel like our show does the same thing." "So I think as long as filmmakers and artists that come on board something all have a clear view of the writing of the action of these scenes, there’s always room for it. I think there’s a lot more road we have to pave, because it’s not like we’ve told every story that involves sex. There’s going to be a lot more. And as long as everyone’s mature enough and smart enough to tell that story properly and make everyone on set feel safe, I think it must still be in shows and movies going forward." "To me, it felt like the blocking of the sex scenes and how those looked, and the intimate scenes, were just as important as the long dialogue scenes. If some people were to think, 'Oh, okay, this is the sex scene. I can just turn off my brain and close my eyes', they would be missing the story and a lot of the communication that goes from point A to point B. In every single intimate scene in the show, we start somewhere different than where we end." 🔗 https://www.teenvogue.com/story/pre-fame-hudson-williams-connor-storrie-interview-heated-rivalry

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 13:28:36
Hudson Williams on Shane Hollander's inner struggle's in "Heated Rivalry" (via @TeenVogue) "There’s a scene in episode six that I think is hitting the nail on the head for what Shane’s struggle is. Everything needs to be perfect. It needs to be dialed in. I thought there was a comparison to be made with a Sidney Crosby or a Kobe Bryant in the sense that everything has to be regimented. I think, yeah, part of the internalized homophobia — [his sexuality] doesn’t fit his idea of 'perfect', because of his own internal biases that he might’ve not even recognized. I guess he adopted [these biases] without really consciously choosing to adopt these beliefs. So I think being perfect and being gay for some reason — for Shane, those two things don’t fit. So the first time he falls in love is with Ilya, there’s so many things to reconcile that just drive him insane. So I think the presentation of perfection and then really the entire story of Heated Rivalry, which is this romance — those two things for Shane are on opposite ends due to his own hangups."

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 13:24:56
Hudson Williams on who he thinks fell first and harder, Shane or Ilya, to @TeenVogue "In the book, Ilya sees Shane’s freckles. There’s something about it that’s immediately alluring, and I think Ilya has less walls up in the sense that he can just kind of be okay with that feeling arising, whether or not it leads to infatuation or something. But I think there’s something a little bit more chaotically kinetic in Shane because there’s the intense sort of attraction. He doesn’t know what it is. But then there’s also the internalized homophobia that just wants to punch whatever it is back down and into place. I think that sends Shane into a little bit more of a panic."

original tweet

Sophie Nélisse on how welcoming and at ease she felt working alongside Hudson Williams on ‘Heated Rivalry’ (via matinastevis) “I owe it also a lot to Hudson. He has this kind of voice that I always said that I kinda want him to be on my Headspace app and be the meditation audio guide that I listen to when going to sleep. And that voice is the same way how he is with people; he is so generous, he is so calming, he is so present.”

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-30 07:04:16
New article about the Prime Time screen and media industry conference by The New York Times: “The world knows that Shane and Ilya are rising hockey stars who fall for each other as they face off in one of the greatest rivalries the game has ever known but they’re also two young men who are terrified of being their fullest selves, and we live in an increasingly dangerous, divided and intolerant world.” The audience went completely silent. “A fundamental Canadian value is that people should be able to be whoever they want to be, to love whoever they want to love,” Mr. Carney said to roaring applause in his keynote speech at the entertainment-industry event. (Photo by Andrej Ivanov/Getty Images) 🔗 https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/30/world/canada/heated-rivalry-carney-canada.html

Margot Robbie and director Emerald Fennell mentioned Heated Rivalry in a recent interview: E: You haven't seen it yet? M: No! I've kinda of been saving it for myself. […] It's kinda a slice of cake. 2026-01-28 19:14:16
Hudson Williams on his versatility as an actor and directors referencing his short films during castings "I think I was trying to show another side of myself [separate from Shane] after ‘Heated Rivalry’. I have a cocky side – ‘Fuck you, this is me’ – but I also have a soft, quiet, scared side. I decided to show that. Some directors have seen my short films and were like, ‘Oh.’ I never thought they’d reference those films as a reason to cast me. I made them for two dollars and a camcorder." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

Hudson Williams on being on @FallonTonight "It was very surreal. I kinda felt like I was on one of those fake replications of a talk show, (...) that's what it felt like because I couldn't possibly believe I'm actually on Jimmy Fallon. Definitely a surreal moment." 🔗 https://youtu.be/WDoFq428QBw

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 18:25:23
Hudson Williams on walking for the Dsquared2 fashion show and the critism he faced (via @wonderlandmag) "I was the worst walker on the runway (...) because it felt daunting. In the moment, I didn’t really feel scared. I was just like, ‘Fuck.’ All the other guys had heels and platforms. I only had flat boots, because I couldn’t walk. They were normal, flat-ass boots. But then they buttoned a ski-boot ankle warmer on, so I couldn’t move my ankle. My ankle did not articulate. I didn’t practise my walk." "I don’t really get nervous before these things. I only get mad afterwards that I didn’t get nervous. So I was like, ‘Fuck, I would have practised if I knew I was going to get shat on that hard.’ And I saw people just go, ‘Who the fuck is this guy?’ And I was like…I know. I know." "Some people were like, ‘Face card, no walk card.’ I had so much salt. I was trying to [channel] models who do high fashion, the stiff, robot walk. But then Dsquared2 is a fun show where you’re meant to swag it up. And I was like, I could have swagged it up. I could have walked with umph." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 18:09:13
Hudson Williams on how he's staying grounded and navigating his rise to fame (via @wonderlandmag) "I’ve been writing a lot. I have two separate journals. One is a manuscript – semi-autobiographical – about this period in my life, where I blur the lines between fiction and real life. Then I have one where I just recount things: I met this person today, this happened. And I have a Google Doc where I’m building my internal monologue, writing down what I’m thinking and feeling in hotel rooms between events. It’s really fun to write. There’s this writer I love, Joan Didion, and she once said she journals so that when she gets really old, she can pick up her books and find her way back to herself again. That inspired me. It also helps with gratitude. I can look back and go, ‘Wow, that really blew me away.’" "Keeping grounded, I have such a good group of friends. They don’t care too much. They’re like, ‘Okay, good for you. This is amazing. But what’s your next role?’ Or, ‘Are you treating us well? Are you treating the people around you well?’ That’s make or break. How you show up as a person matters more than anything else. I use my friends and family as a metric. Am I being an asshole? Let’s make sure I’m staying kind." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 18:00:55
Hudson Williams on being clipped for viral headlines (via @wonderlandmag) "Sometimes I’m just talking for two hours in these interviews, and I loosen up and say some things. And then those are the bits that get run. One thing I said was like, ‘I hope I’m an inspiration to all these Asian kids out there.’ And I made a joke, like, ‘Asian kids that are going to get railed,’ and then they combined it and made it a fucking headline. I was like, ‘What the hell?’" 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:58:33
Hudson Williams talks about Golden Globes, @omarapollo and @charli_xcx for @wonderlandmag

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:57:58
"Right now? I feel out of shape. I’ve expended everything. All Hudson had to give, Hudson gave. I need rest. I need movies and books. Artists are sponges. You soak things up and pour them into work. These events feel like preparation for things I don’t even want to do. I love fashion, but no one’s handing me a needle and asking me to make a dress. I want to act, direct, write – and I have no time for that. Schedules are 9am to 9pm, sometimes 8am to 11.30pm. You get an hour here, 30 minutes there. You can’t settle. You’re just absorbing toxic shit on your phone. There’s no routine, and routine breeds mental structure. I don’t have that right now, and it sucks." - Hudson Williams 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:55:56
Hudson Williams talking about this picture with Connor Storrie by @wonderlandmag Check whole video: https://youtu.be/WDoFq428QBw?si=zl9SUvyhbBZrmt_7

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:53:06
Hudson Williams on his upcoming future plans (via @wonderlandmag) "I’ve got projects in the inbox. I want to do a few things before season two. I want to break the idea of Shane. I love him, but I don’t want every role to be ‘square guy in a romance’. I want to do other stuff. Eventually, I want to do theatre. Broadway. A musical, after singing lessons. There’s a lot I want to do in the next two years." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:47:14
Hudson Williams photographed by Davis Bates for @wonderlandmag #4

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:44:43
Hudson Williams confirms he is 5’11.7 in height (via @wonderlandmag) "Since tenth grade, I used to say 5’10 and a half, because that was the last time I measured. Since then, I was just saying the wrong height. I also wanted to be humble. I didn’t want to be one of those guys who’s like ‘six foot’ and isn’t actually six foot. And then my mum was like, ‘You seem taller. You’re as tall as your dad, who’s six foot.’ So I stood next to the wall, and I’m 5’11.7”. With my hair, I’m past six foot. I’m not going to lie. I’m going to say what my fucking height is. And people can go, ‘No, he’s not. He’s lying.’ But I don’t care. That’s my actual fucking height." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:42:17
@PopCrave YES HE DID

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:41:32
Hudson Williams on what he didn't expect when rising to fame (via @wonderlandmag) "I didn’t think there would be as many disrespectful people as there are. And vice versa, I didn’t think there would be as many respectful people as there are. I thought everyone would just be screaming, nice, or kind of like, ‘Oh my God.’ But there are people who are like, ‘No, let’s be respectful to him and his private life. Let’s not share these photos. When we approach him on the street, let’s be mindful of appropriate times.’ I’m seeing those comments, and that’s cool. But then there are disrespectful people who are just like, ‘Fuck that guy.’ And I’m like, holy shit. Why are people hating on me? There’s also all this fake news about me. And I’m like, ‘How do I already have fake news?’" 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:38:26
Hudson Williams by @wonderlandmag!

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:37:25
Hudson Williams on going viral for smoking in Milan (via @wonderlandmag) "I’m trying to quit. But that’s another thing I got called out for. People were like, ‘Look at him aura farming.’ I want to point out right now: I was going to smoke that fucking cigarette outside Armani, and my brand agent, Leilani, was like, ‘You should probably just smoke it now, because you won’t have time over there.’ And I was like, ‘Right here?’ So again, I was like, ‘Okay.’ And then some people said I wasn’t even inhaling, that I was just blowing out smoke. Bitch, I was walking so fucking fast, I was breathing heavy. Obviously not every drag is…[breathes very deeply]. Believe me, I needed that nicotine to calm down." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:35:38
@outofstyle1331 @wonderlandmag Refresh the page! It was for me and I got it!

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 17:30:46
Hudson Williams on working with Connor Storrie and them actively persuing separate promo opportunities (via @wonderlandmag) "Connor and I are aligned. We want to be different artists. We don’t want to be the Olsen twins. We want to be Connor and Hudson, with different filmographies and different deals. As much as we love each other, our friendship doesn’t need to be public. Jacob [Tierney] has that quote: ‘Shane and Ilya are for the public. Connor and Hudson are for themselves.’ And that’s how we feel. People want to see us front row together all the time, but we actively avoid that, because it starts to feel like branding. If you’re always seen together, you become attached at the hip. I FaceTime him whenever I’m free. But if we’re offered the same fashion shows, we’re like, ‘Which one are you going to? Okay, I’ll go to a different one.’ We’re stubborn, selfish artists who want to be our own people. We’re like, ‘I love you. I don’t want to do everything with you.’" 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

2026-01-28 17:25:58
Hudson Williams confirms the infamous Letterboxd standal was fabricated HW: "There was a Letterboxd scandal. Have you heard about this? So fucking many Letterboxd reviews of mine that aren’t mine. I’ve seen one real Letterboxd review, and maybe like 25 fake ones." SN: "And are they bad reviews?" HW: "Some of them are good, but they’re stupid. Some of them are really awful. And I’m like, I like that movie." 🔗 https://www.wonderlandmagazine.com/2026/01/28/everybodys-thirsty-for-hudson-williams/

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 14:07:40
Jacob Tierney and Brendan Brady made sure that all “Heated Rivalry” cast members earn profit from sales of merch items that use their likeness, a practice that is rare in the film and television industry 🔗 https://www.theglobeandmail.com/culture/film-and-tv/television/article-viral-heated-rivalry-canada-fleece-is-about-to-become-a-reality/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 13:56:17
Jacob Tierney and Brendan Brady discuss the upcoming Shane Hollander Canada fleece, “Heated Rivalry” merch store, a potential collaboration with the NHL, and more with @globeandmail “We’re so excited to partner with Province of Canada. As a Canadian-based, independent apparel brand that does amazing work sourcing and manufacturing high-quality clothing, we share a common ethos. We look forward to bringing it to life with the creativity and care fans expect.” - Jacob “It was really important for me that this be manufactured in Canada. I hope the fans will be very happy. We have been trying to make this a priority and to live up to the values we want with this show.” - Brendan They also expect to start re-stocking items from the official “Heated Rivalry” merch store by mid February 🔗 Read more here: https://www.theglobeandmail.com/culture/film-and-tv/television/article-viral-heated-rivalry-canada-fleece-is-about-to-become-a-reality/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-28 13:39:30
The Shane Hollander Canada fleece from “Heated Rivalry” is getting an official release! It will be made in Canada with 10% of all sales being donated to an yet-to-be-announced LGBTQ+ charity. International shipping will be available. Release date is to be confirmed. 🔗 Sign up for updates here: https://provinceofcanada.com/pages/heated-rivalry

“Heated Rivalry” casting director Jenny Lewis on casting Hudson Williams as Shane Hollander “With Hudson, (…) it just felt different to me. He just spoke emotionally. (…) I was just like, ‘This is staying with me.’ I couldn’t get him out of my head.” 🔗 https://theankler.com/p/copy-heated-rivalry-casting-directors-storrie-williams-jenny-lewis-sara-kay

Jonah de Simone, Shane Hollander’s hockey body double talks about his experience filming “Heated Rivalry” and how it was working with Hudson Williams; “He says the actor was receptive to learn the mannerisms of a hockey player. They had to ensure everything was identical between them for scenes where Jonah stepped in.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/IcX88wTK0j8?si=voL0_qoy1SS-1jGu

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-23 21:40:20
‘Heated Rivalry’ costume designer Hanna Puley on Shane Hollander’s white suit and what it represents for him (via @badhombremag_) “The appearance of Shane’s white suit comes after he comes out of the closet [to] Rose; it’s almost an immediate reaction to accepting a more authentic identity. However, instead of externally embodying a visibly queer identity, [he] simply reverses his usual look: the black suit with white shirt becomes a white suit with a dark shirt. It is a small step towards what will later be a more authentic representation of [himself] and an exploration of how [he] wants to be perceived.” 🔗 Read the full interview here: https://badhombremagazine.com/hana-puley-y-el-vestuario-que-hizo-arder-heated-rivalry/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-23 21:20:40
Costume designer Hanna Puley on Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov’s fashion evolution in “Heated Rivalry” “At the end of the season, when they're alone together in the cabin, they almost revert to a more younger/childlike version of themselves. There's no effort to impress each other, just to exist. Sharing clothes makes their mutual comfort evident. They're both at their most mature, finally able to share the secret they've carried for nearly a decade and to exist together without any pretense.” 🔗 Full interview: https://badhombremagazine.com/hana-puley-y-el-vestuario-que-hizo-arder-heated-rivalry/

BC Children’s Hospital created a fundraiser in honor of Hudson Williams’ birthday. There’s truly no better way to celebrate him than by giving back to the hospital that saved his life - a place that holds deep meaning for him and his family. Hudson has also supported the hospital through his own donations in the past, making this gesture even more special. 🔗 https://fundraise.bcchf.ca/fundraisers/HudsonWilliams/hudson--s-birthday-fundraiser--

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-22 17:57:04
Christina Chang (Yuna) talks about Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @THR “It was wonderful working with Hudson, Huddy. He is so easy and relaxed, in general, and he’s funny. They’re both clowns, if I’m being honest. He’s really easy to work with and I’m looking forward to more clowning, is the short answer.” “They deserve all the flowers, so I’m really happy to see that they are having this experience. I continue to say to them when we speak or text, “Just make sure that you’re hydrating and sleeping and taking care of yourself.” I couldn’t be more happy for them. This couldn’t have happened to nicer guys. They’re truly kind and thoughtful people.” 🔗 https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-yuna-season-2-interview-1236476468/

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-22 00:28:47
‘Heated Rivalry’ intimacy coordinator Chala Hunter talks about Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie in latest interview (via @accesshollywood) “They’re both such talented, kind, wonderful people. They’re both such true (...) they both are so devoted to their craft and they’re genuinely like people with so much integrity. They’re both very charming, very funny, like really real people as well. I feel like both [are] very honest people, if that makes sense. Like no façade, super genuine. I think they both have a silly side, which I feel like the world has already seen. And both genuinely just very kind. They were both so lovely to work with, but also just like always very respectful and kind to like everyone on the set. To me, they’re just standout guys, what can I say?” 🔗 Watch the full interview here: https://youtu.be/Riw2qWUWmHQ

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-20 21:08:21
We never post pictures of his family or his personal life unless he shares them himself. We also do not post content from gossips pages, rumors about his personal life or candid photos/videos taken without his knowledge, unless it is during a public event, such as the Golden Globes or the Variety Party, where he is aware he will be photographed. We are also aware of the presence of stalkers who leak personal content. While some of that information reaches us, we are very careful not to post it or report it publicly, as we do not want to spread it or give attention to it. Also we receive pictures with fans and they ask us to not post. We try our best to create a safe space for Hudson and the fans.

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-20 18:29:43
Hudson Williams talks about his future aspirations in acting, directing and writing (via @evanrosskatz) “I think it’s inevitable within the next 5 years I have my feature debut, which I probably will have wrote and just the egoomaniac in me is going to act in it. Because it doesn’t feel like it comes from a place of conceit at least. It feels like it comes from a place of, I have a specific tone I like when I write. Selfishly, I enjoy what I write and it feels like the only person who writes like me is me. Whether or not it’s great or will be appreciated or anyone will think it’s good, I have no control of or no prospects of even possibly understanding. But I like it and so I want to exist in the worlds I create.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/sVP2v04K7K8

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-20 17:05:55
‘Heated Rivalry’ has been nominated for Best TV Drama for the 2026 Queerties (via @Variety) The Queerties is a pop culture awards show where “queer people honor the best of the best, voting for the talent and creatives who inspire and pave the way for the next generation of queer youth.” Public voting opens January 20, with fans able to vote once per day in each category through February 17. Winners will be revealed in March. 🔗 Read more: https://variety.com/2026/awards/awards/heated-rivalry-queerties-nominations-2026-1236630684/

2026-01-20 16:39:54
Hudson Williams via by.aikaflores Instagram story

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-20 16:18:59
Hudson Williams reacts to Donatella Versace watching ‘Heated Rivalry’ and wanting to go to the cottage (via @evanrosskatz) “I blame Jacob Tierney for not making a world in which that could have happen.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/sVP2v04K7K8

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-20 16:10:50
Hudson Williams on fans waiting outside his hotel and un-solicited pictures being taken of him at airports (via @evanrosskatz) “It's not easy having paparazzi, people constantly [asking for photos]. Although it feels good that people admire your work...We can go to pockets of places I never thought we would be found and we're still pulled aside for a photo.” “These people seem very nice but I can't possibly sign and talk to everyone, there's an element of selfishness, because if I was to stay and sign everything, the security, they have other jobs to do, my publicists and agents need to get somewhere, it can be a selfish act and not a generous one to stand there and meet everyone. There are other people around me.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/sVP2v04K7K8

2026-01-19 17:04:15
A fans interaction with Hudson Williams today (via EleonoraCo76325) “At a certain point, walking literally right behind him (he’s over 1.80 meters tall), he turns around and says to us, in the most puppy-dog way possible, ‘I'm so stressed’. And we just replied to him, ‘I'm so sorry’. Because honestly, the whole walk had been a surreal madness and he, with the nonchalance in the world, replied to him ‘nono you're fine’ and he was cracking up — it's an indescribable cuteness”

2026-01-19 01:18:54
HOLD YOUR BACK (2026), also producer “Hudson Williams is the reason this movie exists. I had an idea about two men going through a violent yet passionate breakup, and he filled me with enough passion and help to go through with its creation. So with his newfound and well deserved recognition, I figured why not give the world more of the brilliant Hudson. We got many more movies and stories to come, and I’m beyond stoked to start sharing them.” 🔗 https://youtu.be/O1ebmWVH8g4

HEATED RIVALRY (2025-) as Shane Hollander Two rival hockey stars, Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov, compete on rival teams, the Montreal Metros and the Boston Raiders, respectively. Although their on-ice rivalry is amplified by media coverage and public perception, the two develop a private, initially casual sexual relationship that develops into something more over several years. 🔗 Crave: https://www.crave.ca/en/series/heated-rivalry-59137 🔗 HBO Max: https://www.hbomax.com/shows/heated-rivalry/50cd4e99-04ee-427b-a3b4-da721ed05d9c

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-19 01:13:05
SNOW ANGEL (2025) as Trevor, also director “With ‘Snow Angel’, we wanted to create a character study where the main character is our vessel for exploring morality in an immoral community of drug running. Stripping away any fancy set pieces, our goal was to explore unsolvable questions by putting them in a high-tension crock pot. After finishing it I felt I failed in lots of ways creatively with it, so it took some time to look at it with affection. And now I do! I have a lot of love for it for all the things it taught me and all the people I got to make it with.” Filmed May 2022 and was his debut short film as a director! 🔗 https://youtu.be/_-_KoZGurVU

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-16 23:56:17
Hudson Williams talking to @voguemagazine before walking at Dsquared2’s fashion show in Milan today “It’s nerve wracking, I feel very out of my element. This doesn’t feel like acting. So I’m just going to try my best to walk and serve as well as all these other people doing it. But I’m sure I’ll look a little like a chicken out there - like Bambi.” “I love my look. I’m really comfortable and confident in it, and it makes me feel like I can put a little more oomph in my walk.” #HudsonDsquared2 #HudsonMFW https://www.vogue.com/article/zero-pucks-given-hudson-williams-on-his-runway-debut-at-dsquared2

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-16 21:32:20
"Hi, mama. Love you. My question is if stereotypes play anything in your acting journey and if you like embracing some certain Asian archetypes and playing them, being that you're part-Asian - or if that most of your interest is just playing characters that have nothing to do with Asian, and how that comes into your work? Love you, mama. Bye-bye." Hudson Williams sent a video to Christina Chang through Forbes Magazine’s interview. @Forbes release the video! 😭 Check her answer: https://www.forbes.com/sites/jeffconway/2026/01/16/heated-rivalry-actress-christina-chang-talks-season-2-and-her-career/

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-15 13:36:08
Connor Storrie says that his audition instincts saying Hudson Williams should play Shane Hollander were constantly validated throughout filming “Heated Rivalry” “I feel like it wasn’t even on set that that was validated, because I think we had the acting down. I mean, I know this is about the acting, but I think it felt validated just with the natural flow of things. We had such a natural flow that, you know, everyone talks about the chemistry, the chemistry — and that really starts by just trusting each other.” “I don’t think people could have the sort of on-screen chemistry that Hudson and I have without genuinely embracing each other in real life and really feeling seen and heard and safe. That’s when it felt validated and real to me.” 🔗 https://www.today.com/popculture/tv/connor-storrie-heated-rivalry-interview-season-2-rachel-reid-rcna254003

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-14 19:56:11
Ksenia Daniela (Svetlana) talks to @NylonMag about working with Hudson Williams, and how she wants her character to meet Shane Hollander in S2 “I remember having such a fun time during the gala scenes. I remember even though Hudson [Williams] wasn’t in those scenes, he was still there on set the whole day, taking [behind the scenes] of us and messing around. I was glad he was there because otherwise, I don’t ever get to see him. We’d see each other passing by when we would have scenes on the same day. I really liked that he would come and watch the process. I might do that next season when there’s a fun scene I’m not involved in.” “Jacob [Tierney] could really do whatever he wants with the next season, but me personally, what I would want to see Svetlana meet Shane. They could do so much with them meeting: two sides of Ilya’s world and the two people he trusts the most. I want to see Shane and Ilya’s more tender, sweet, couply moments that we didn’t get to see much of in Season 1. We got to see it a little bit in the cottage, but not as much in the rest of the series. I’d also love to see some of the female characters meet, if that would make sense in that universe. I want to see Svetlana involved in some hockey.” 🔗 https://www.nylon.com/entertainment/ksenia-daniela-kharlamova-heated-rivalry-svetlana-interview

2026-01-13

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-13 23:23:00
Christina Chang (Yuna) reflecting back on filming the finale of ‘Heated Rivalry’ with Hudson Williams, Connor Storrie and Dylan Walsh (David): “I have so much fun with those boys. They’re like my sons on set, but also my friends off set. I don’t want to make it sound like, “Yeah, it was really easy,” because at the end of the day, we’re all very focused, and we do our work when we need to do our work. But we’re kind of clowns, and it was just the four of us for a couple days, so it was a really wonderful experience. It sounds boring and cliché, but it really was. It was towards the end of the shoot, and I think we all felt like, “We get to do this, finally.” I know for the boys, they had so much tension, and Ilya had to be so closed off all the time, so it felt like a breath of fresh air. Everyone could just be together and shoot these scenes that we were all waiting for. What everyone was waiting to watch, we couldn’t wait to film — [that] is the best way to put it, I guess.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.tvguide.com/news/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-yuna-and-shane-finale/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-13 23:20:51
Christina Chang on how protective she feels of her co-stars Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie after the show’s succes: “I do. I do feel protective of them as my castmates and friends and because of my age — [I feel] motherly, I guess maternal love. I think they’ve handled it so well. I have to remind myself that this is the first series they’ve ever done, and that they’re only 25. I’m not saying [their age] is why they’re handling it well. They’re just handling it well, period, regardless of whether they're 45 or 25. But I’m more impressed that they’re such young men, and they’re so nice. They actually are really nice guys, and fun and funny. I think they’re exhausted. I know that. We check in via text. [Checks her phone.] In fact, I just got a text from Hudson right now. But I’m always checking in on them, and their schedules are crazy right now, and my hope for them is that they can sleep soon. I want them to rest, and I want to make them chicken soup. [Laughs.] But, really, I’m like, ‘Hudson, when are you back in Vancouver? When can I have you over for dinner? And you can just chill.’” 🔗 Read the full article here: https://www.tvguide.com/news/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-yuna-and-shane-finale/

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-13 23:13:00
Christina Chang on filming two versions of Yuna and Shane’s post-coming out conversation scene in the ‘Heated Rivalry’ finale: “On the day, we were shooting on location, and Jacob was basically like, “OK, don’t hate me, but I think I’m going to rewrite the whole thing.” And we were like, “What?!” So he doesn’t quickly just write it right there [in front of us], but he gets us the pages as soon as possible. Hudson and I started rehearsing the scene in between other takes of the table [scenes], running lines and things like that. I will say that [the final cut] was a much more distilled version, and Jacob was trying to get to the heart of what the [original] scene had. The other scene was beautiful, but there were more words. It was a longer scene, and this one just got right down to it and was also a little bit more colloquial, if you will. The other one was beautifully written. This one sounded more like something we would say, maybe.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.tvguide.com/news/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-yuna-and-shane-finale/

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-12 23:12:29
#GoldenGlobes Producers explain when they realized they needed to have ‘Heated Rivalry’ stars Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie at the award show: “‘Heated Rivalry’ has been on our radar. We’ve been tracking it for about our whole production period. But I can’t even begin to tell you how broad the lobbying became. And when I say lobbying, I just mean conversations with people of certain demographics, certain age groups, and realizing how hot this was becoming. It became something obvious to us that, especially because they haven’t appeared anywhere before in our universe, this was the perfect opportunity. And I’m really happy that they were able to work out getting in the country and all that. It was a really cool moment.” 🔗 Read more here: https://variety.com/2026/tv/news/golden-globes-producers-show-behind-the-scenes-1236629717/

2026-01-12 15:25:38
Official synopsis for upcoming Hollanov book, titled ‘Unrivaled’, out September 2026 “They’ve gotten a lot of love from fans who are thrilled for them. But some people in the hockey world are still reeling from their relationship reveal, and the backlash — led by popular hockey podcast Top Shelf and the #TakeBackHockey movement — is getting louder. Ilya and Shane are finally able to stand together in the light, the way they’d always wanted. And now they might be facing their biggest challenge yet.” 🔗 https://people.com/heated-rivalry-author-rachel-reid-announces-new-game-changers-book-unrivaled-exclusive-11882332

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-12 15:21:59
Rachel Reid officially announces her new ‘Game Changers’ book titled “Unrivalled”, which follows on from “The Long Game” (via @people) “I'm really excited to have the chance to continue [the Hollanov] story with Unrivaled. I’m writing this one for all the Hollanov fans, old and new. I can’t tell you how much the love my characters have received over the years means to me. It has been a blast writing these guys again, and revisiting the entire Game Changers cast.”

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-11 17:36:58
“The guest whose arrival caused the sharpest collective inhale was actor Hudson Williams, of TV’s spiciest overnight sensation, Heated Rivalry. (…) This early in the day, Williams’s peers play it (mostly) cool - though Yellowjackets star Jasmin Savoy Brown was pleased to report she just danced between Williams and Storrie at a party two nights ago. ‘There’s a video on my Instagram, if you want to go check it out’, she teased.” - @TheCut on Hudson Williams attending their Golden Globes brunch

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-11 17:33:53
.@TheCut on Hudson Williams attending their Golden Globes brunch yesterday “For Williams, this is all still pretty surreal; his first order of business when he showed up was just to find something to eat. That, he noted, is the craziest part about his sudden fame. ‘There’s so little time to eat’, he said. ‘I’ve only ate two sliders while I’m doing this thing. My hands are shaking because I’m famished. I have scotch.’ Later, when Peoples introduced herself as The Cut’s editor-in-chief, he joked: ‘You didn’t do much editing on my skincare video!’ (Williams was referring to this 20-minute masterpiece, which he called a ‘PSA for fucking ADHD medication.’)” 🔗 https://www.thecut.com/article/heated-rivalry-spiced-up-the-cuts-2026-golden-globes-brunch.html

original tweet

A new ‘Game Changers’ book titled “Unrivaled” by Rachel Reid has appeared on the Barnes & Noble website, with a release date of September 29th 2026 It’s seemingly centred on Shane and Ilya, and follows on from “The Long Game”. Spoilers below for those who have not read the books. Listings with the same ISBN number have also appeared on Target and other retailer websites. 🔗 https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/unrivaled-rachel-reid/1149166585#

2026-01-10 10:48:30
“Heated Rivalry” has been watched for over 600 Million minutes on HBO Max in the US, @nytimes reports “During its debut week on HBO Max, [it] accumulated roughly 30 million streaming minutes.” “By the week of Dec. 26, when the season’s sixth and final episode was released, time spent streaming the show was up more than tenfold, eclipsing 324 million minutes.” “For the week of Jan. 2, the show was viewed for over 254 million minutes.” 🔗 https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/10/business/media/heated-rivalry-hbo-max-popularity.html

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-09 21:01:46
Perhaps it was a typo but the article reads as follows "It's so funny when you get to see these, kind of almost unknowns become household names almost overnight," Fallon noted. "We booked Hudson probably six months ago when the show had some buzz, but it wasn't the biggest thing on everyone's minds, you know?"

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-09 20:49:21
Jimmy Fallon on doing hockey stretches with Hudson Williams during his Tonight Show interview “Let me just tell you something, I did not know what the stretch was going into this. I just knew that he was gonna teach me a stretch. And the photo they sent me had a hockey player with two gloves on the ice, and he was kind of squatting like a catcher at baseball. I go, ‘Do I need to know the stretch?’ And they go, ‘I'd rather you didn't know,’ But I just trust my bit! So then when we went to the floor and I'm like, ‘What is the stretch?’ I saw a little bit. I thought it was like a child pose yoga, you know? He's like, ‘No, it's more like this.’” “Fallon recalled that Williams was ‘in a good mood’ and feeling confident ‘because the audience loved him’, but the host wasn't so confident in his hip stretching skills. ‘I don't even know if my kids can see it’ the comic joked. ‘You almost had to blur something out, I'll say that.’” 🔗 https://ew.com/jimmy-fallon-tonight-show-fan-frenzy-heated-rivalry-hudson-williams-beatlemania-11882565

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-09 20:43:49
Jimmy Fallon compares the crowd during his Hudson Williams interview to The Beatles mania “To have him show up and he's excited — he was in the best mood. He was like, ‘I can't believe this.’ Less than two years ago, he was working at the Old Spaghetti Factory in Canada as a server and now people are waiting in line, screaming his name, and going nuts.” “The whole show was great. But Hudson was the reason everyone was in such a hype mood. Even just mentioning his name, they went wild and I said it's almost deafening. It's almost like seeing those black-and-white videos of the Beatles or something, and you go, ‘How could it be that loud in one room?’” Full article: https://ew.com/jimmy-fallon-tonight-show-fan-frenzy-heated-rivalry-hudson-williams-beatlemania-11882565

2026-01-09 15:20:25
Connor Storrie on presenting at the Golden Globes with Hudson Williams on Sunday (via @THR) “I’m excited. I think that’s a really good platform for Hudson and I to be ourselves. I’m excited that Nikki Glaser is doing it. It’s going to be great.”

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 18:20:18
Hudson Williams was mentioned on The Today Show earlier today: “Last night actor Hudson Williams swung by The Tonight Show [starring] Jimmy Fallon. And if you’re anywhere around here at Thirty Rock, you would’ve thought it was like Beatles-mania! You would have thought BTS was on The Tonight Show, it wasn’t. Look at the crowds here just to catch a glimpse of him walking in the building. That’s impressive.” “By the way, I was at the gym before, like at 5:45, and people were outside.” “By the way, that’s one of those shows that everyone here on the crew has been talking about for two weeks. People are obsessed with it.”

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 16:45:26
Hudson Williams on people speculating about his private life (via SiriusXM) "You can look at whatever I said, I don't care. (...) I'm pretty chill about it. On a scale of freaking me out it's like a 2 out of 10. I'm kinda indifferent [to it] at the end of the day. There are going to be people who have crazy reactions to it, but those people can take a hike because the people who have a healthy reaction are the only people that I care about or respect for having a developed frontal cortex."

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 16:32:06
"Fold me in half, turn me into a pretzel, every position, we didn't hit enough, hopefully in season 2 we can fix that." - Hudson Williams when asked if he had any nerves approaching the intimate scenes in "Heated Rivalry" (via SiriusXM)

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 16:27:41
"I've always liked actors that do subtle emotions really well. I understood that with Shane there needs to be a way to show his emotions, because his voice is kinda capped and restricted, so I had to find a way to bring people in so there not just staring at a brick wall." - Hudson Williams on Shane Hollander's micro expressions (via SiriusXM)

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 16:22:33
"I was tryna steal that and then I forgot to ask wardrobe to give it to me at the end, and I really wanted it. It's still my only regret from season 1." - Hudson Williams on Shane's Canada fleece (via SiriusXM)

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 15:55:11
“Spaghetti sucks, rigatoni is awful. I like a penne noodle.” - Hudson Williams on pasta shapes (Via SiriusXM)

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-08 15:52:32
Hudson Williams on what he hopes fans can take from Shane Hollander’s coming out scene in “Heated Rivalry” (via SiriusXM) “Some people don't have the benefit of having parents who would be as supportive as Shane's. Even with his, there's a deal of clincical-ness to it, it's not the perfect open arms, they're thinking about Reebook [etc.]. But, I hope people can take the feeling that you're not alone, and that's that not something that should be a fantasy, it's what you deserve. Whether that's your parents or not, you deserve a family and a community that puts their arms around you.”

original tweet

Hudson Williams’s previous drama teacher in new Instagram comment “I am bursting with joy-as his teacher, as his colleague and friend, as a fellow Canadian, as a devout and dare I say refined TV connoisseur, and finally as a gay man THIRSTING for gay stories that had authentic love and JOY! (…) Look out Canada we have another bonafide star in the making!!”

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-06 11:49:59
Anthony Meindl’s Actor Workshop, a drama school Hudson Williams attended, via Instagram “🥹 It truly is one of the most beautiful things in life when what the world sees of a person, is what you know to be true. Before Hudson Williams was the Hudson Williams, he was The Hudson Williams to us. A dedicated person, student, member of our community, support system, artist and storyteller. He continually showed up for his fellow actors and community, over and over again. To say he is one of a kind is sort of insane, because he's so much more. He's finally seeing the world recognize him for the crazy dedicated, supportive, loving soul he is and gosh darn it, it's about dang time! Can't wait for season two of Heated Rivalry, and season 3, and season 4...and to watch you skyrocket further. Your success is the culmination of everything you are, and dang boy, we are PROUD over here 💞” Via amawvancouver

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-06 02:49:00
Hanna Puley, Heated Rivalry’s costume designer, also talks about choosing the right pair of glasses on Hudson William for Shane Hollander in new @GQMagazine interview: “[T]he glasses were a real sticking point, and I tried probably about 10 to 15 pairs, especially for Hudson, for Shane. But yeah, for Shane, I had a case full of glasses that we went through because you just have to have them. I remember we tried them on set and had everybody be like, “Those ones.” Yeah, these things are important because they’re in the imagination of the people reading. They’re visualizing it and wanting to get [a pair] that kind of felt classic, but also nerdy in a way, and maybe a little bit outside of that athletic persona of who Shane is.” Read the full interview here: https://www.gq.com/story/heated-rivalry-costume-designer-hanna-puley-interview

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-06 02:33:24
Heated Rivalry’s costume designer, Hanna Puley, talks about how Hudson Williams differs fashion-wise from Shane Hollander in new @GQMagazine interview: “The Shane thing is interesting because I think there’s just so much discomfort in the character himself, in the world outside of being an athlete. Like, Hudson is doing that great ... Hudson and Shane are very different people. What a great thing for him that people are really seeing what a wonderful job he did on screen for the show. Connor, too—very different people than the characters they play. But yeah, Shane, I don’t think we could get him to go much further. And then, cut, I have him in very straight-cut suits, a little bit of tailoring, but nothing crazy, really simple lapels for all the tuxedos. Ilya’s tuxedos, we did double-breasted and peak lapel and different colors and played around with that a little bit more. I think it’s all just about trying to tap into the real character beats. Shane, I think, just doesn’t exist in the world that way.” Read more here: https://www.gq.com/story/heated-rivalry-costume-designer-hanna-puley-interview

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 18:37:16
Just wanted to have a quick conversation with everyone about something we’ve been noticing lately. The fact that some smaller fake accounts are disrespecting Hudson DOES NOT give anyone permission to say harmful or disrespectful things about Connor Storrie to protect Hudson or anyone. We don’t need to respond with attacks — please ignore or report instead. Hudson would not be happy seeing this kind of behavior. Two best friends joking around is not a license to disrespect Connor, push narratives, or cross boundaries. Any form of disrespect toward him will not be tolerated on this page. Hudson loves Connor, and so do we. At the same time, no one should be attacking Hudson, creating narratives about his personal life, or parasocially overanalyzing their interactions to suggest that he’s making his best friend uncomfortable on camera. They are both adults. We already know they are very close, comfortable with each other in different ways, and that no one is uncomfortable. If there were ever an issue in the future, that would be for them to discuss privately and resolve - as the adult best friends they are.

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 17:39:04
Old news but too cute to not share 🥹 https://www.wmagazine.com/culture/connor-storie-heated-rivalry-finale-interview

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 17:38:21
Connor Storrie on navigating his new-found fame alongside Hudson Williams (via wmagazine) “Hudson is my best friend, and I literally can’t fathom doing this without him. We were both in similar places in life before this. We quit our jobs within a day of each other. We booked this and flew out the same day. Now it’s turning into two people who are being seen internationally for the first time. This has been the highlight of my life, and meeting Hudson makes that 10 times sweeter.”

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 17:13:48
https://www.behindtheblinds.be/love/too-big-to-contain

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 17:10:34
Connor Storrie mentions Hudson Williams during his Behind The Blinds interview “As an actor, Hudson is incredibly intentional and thoughtful. I’m more playful and experimental – I don’t always take things seriously enough. He holds me accountable. Seeing his dedication makes me want to step up my game.” “He’s one of the only people who can match my enthusiasm. We ‘yes-and’ each other constantly. We have this irreverent way of joking about life because the situation we’re in is so bizarre. It came out of nowhere. Being able to laugh at the ridiculousness and the beauty of it together – that’s really special.”

original tweet

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-05 16:28:10
Argentinian Olympic field hockey player Nicolás Keenan, who’s engaged to Dutch politician Rob Jetten, recently posted about “Heated Rivalry” “at episode 3... this is definitely the one I've liked and related to the most so far. Secret love behind four walls... been there, done that. Not letting Rob come to my games. Not letting him be part of my socials. Passing him on the street and acting like he was a stranger. Watching it now in the show... yeah, not proud of it.” “will continue watching tonight! 🤗” Via nicokeenan on Instagram Stories

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-01 22:12:29
“[Hudson] is the reason this movie exists. I had an idea about two men going through a violent yet passionate breakup up, and he filled me with enough passion and help to go through with its creation. So with his newfound and well deserved recognition, I figured why not give the world more of the brilliant Hudson. We got many more movies and stories to come, and I’m beyond stoked to start sharing them. ” (via zackfonzovs on Instagram) Watch “Hold Your Back” out NOW!!! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1ebmWVH8g4 TW: abuse, blood, intimate partner violence

✨ @hudsonwupdates
2026-01-01 00:11:31
In some places it’s already new years so let me get emotional and thank Jacob Tierney first for being the #1st Hudson Williams fan ever and insisting to have him in HR so we could all meet the amazing, unhinged, funny and smart person he is. 2025 was a life changing year for him and for all of us, LIVES has been changed since we met him through with the sunshine he carries on his chest. And the only thing I have in my mind it’s JUST the beginning of everything for him! Of many projects, movies, photoshoots and we will be here to help him make ALL of his dreams come true! Thank you all for making so many things possible for that little guy recording short films aside and serving table at Old Spaghetti. And thank you Hudson for being exactly who you are. Never ever change. HAPPY NEW YEAR! 🥳

original tweet
